articles.title: Prediction Markets Are Turning Into a Bot Playground
articles.summary: Automated trading bots are flooding prediction markets, executing thousands of micro-trades and exploiting tiny price inefficiencies faster than human traders can blink. This algorithmic arms race will either make markets more efficient by tightening spreads, or create a two-tiered system where retail bettors get consistently outmaneuvered by sophisticated trading algorithms.

articles.title: Why Does The CFTC Chair Want People To Bet On Drug Trials At Prediction Markets?
articles.summary: CFTC Chair Rostin Behnam is pushing for prediction markets on pharmaceutical trial outcomes, letting traders bet on whether experimental drugs will pass clinical trials. If regulators greenlight drug trial betting, it could unlock a massive new market while giving pharma companies real-time pricing signals on their pipeline value.

articles.title: You can bet on climate disasters. Business is booming
articles.summary: Climate catastrophe bonds and disaster prediction markets are exploding as extreme weather becomes more frequent and expensive, letting investors bet on hurricanes, floods, and wildfires. The money flowing into these markets signals that smart money thinks climate disasters are becoming more predictable, creating new opportunities for anyone willing to bet against Mother Nature's tantrums.

articles.title: Prediction markets watch
articles.summary: Prediction markets are gaining mainstream attention as news outlets and analysts start tracking their signals more seriously. This legitimizes betting markets as information sources and could drive more liquidity from institutions who previously dismissed them as gambling.

articles.title: Wall Street Is Already Betting on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Wall Street firms are placing bets on prediction market platforms themselves, treating these forecasting venues as investment opportunities rather than just trading tools. This institutional money validates prediction markets as a legitimate asset class and will likely fuel rapid expansion, better liquidity, and higher-stakes political and economic betting.

articles.title: Traders Flip Senate Control Bet as Democrats Overtake Republicans on Kalshi, Polymarket
articles.summary: Prediction market traders are now betting Democrats will control the Senate after the 2024 elections, with the party overtaking Republicans as favorites on both Kalshi and Polymarket. The flip signals either late-breaking campaign momentum or smart money recognizing value in previously underpriced Democratic odds.

articles.title: Polymarket Five-Minute Bets Capture ‘Addictive’ Crypto Craze
articles.summary: Polymarket launched five-minute betting windows that are hooking crypto traders with rapid-fire prediction fixes. The move signals how prediction markets are morphing into gambling-adjacent products that prioritize user engagement over forecasting accuracy.

articles.title: AI agents are quietly rewriting prediction market trading
articles.summary: Autonomous AI systems are now placing bets and executing trades across prediction markets without human oversight, fundamentally changing how these platforms operate. This shift means human traders are increasingly competing against algorithms that can process information faster, trade around the clock, and potentially manipulate prices in ways that weren't possible before.

articles.title: Mojtaba Khamenei To Be Removed Before 2026 Ends? Here's What Crypto Prediction Market Says About Iran's New Supreme Leader
articles.summary: Crypto prediction markets are betting on whether Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's newly appointed Supreme Leader, will get ousted before 2026 wraps up. The action suggests traders think Iran's power transition won't be smooth, creating volatile odds on Middle East geopolitical events that smart money can capitalize on.

articles.title: San Jose mayor behind in polls but ahead on prediction markets
articles.summary: San Jose's incumbent mayor is trailing in traditional polling but bettors are backing them to win reelection, creating a classic disconnect between public sentiment and market wisdom. This gap suggests either the polls are missing something crucial about voter turnout and late-deciding voters, or prediction market participants are about to learn an expensive lesson about local politics.

articles.title: With boom in prediction markets, some lawmakers worry about how to police themselves
articles.summary: Lawmakers are grappling with how to regulate prediction markets while potentially trading on them themselves, creating obvious conflicts of interest. The regulatory uncertainty could either legitimize these markets through proper oversight or kill their momentum if politicians overreact to avoid looking like hypocrites.

articles.title: Kharg Island no longer under Iranian control by March 31?...
articles.summary: Iran's Kharg Island, which handles 90% of the country's oil exports, faces potential loss of government control by March 31 according to prediction markets. Oil futures and geopolitical betting markets will swing wildly if this major export terminal actually changes hands, given Kharg processes roughly 1.6 million barrels daily.
